summary: Investigator ran a thorough, negative-result audit of the TabM repo against the four prompt-specified concerns plus a leakage-taxonomy sweep, each refuted by concrete file:line citations, and correctly declined to fabricate a fix.

payload.summary: Investigator ran a thorough, negative-result audit of the TabM repo against the four prompt-specified concerns plus a leakage-taxonomy sweep, each refuted by concrete file:line citations, and correctly declined to fabricate a fix.

checks.label: hypothesis_coverage
checks.note: Five hypotheses cover the four prompt-targeted concerns (aggregation, k-sweep, hyperparameter traceability, param-count) plus a generic leakage check; appropriate for a 'is there actually a bug?' audit on a well-maintained repo.

checks.label: evidence_quality
checks.note: Every Finding cites specific file:line locations (e.g. paper/bin/model.py:536-543, paper/lib/data.py:284, paper/lib/deep.py:355-356) and quotes the matched code semantics rather than paraphrasing.

checks.label: fix_minimality
checks.note: No diff was applied; the investigator explicitly refused to fabricate a fix in a clean repo, which is the correct minimal action when no defect exists.

checks.label: causal_link
checks.note: No metric delta was claimed; the 'Remaining uncertainty' section makes clear no numerical re-run occurred, so there is no spurious causal claim to verify.

checks.label: alternative_explanations
checks.note: The verdict of 'no bug' is supported by independently ruling out each of five distinct failure modes; a standard taxonomy sweep (leakage, group splits, duplicates, temporal, metric mismatch) is explicitly noted as producing no hits.

checks.label: uncertainty_honesty
checks.note: Five substantive caveats listed, including the un-rerun metrics, un-verified k-sweep report.json contents, the `_init_first_adapter` historical-artifact comment, unaudited TabReD split script, and absence of subgroup slicing — these are real limitations, not padding.

checks.label: suggested_followup
checks.note: Open one `report.json` under `paper/exp/tabm/*/0-ensemble-5/` and cross-check its headline metric against the k-sensitivity table in §5.3 — this is the cheapest way to confirm the k-sweep numbers actually trace to disk, which this audit explicitly deferred.
